title: Python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, SDL, Initialization, and the Main Loop · Contract Workshop
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.
Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.
Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Choice
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 2851 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Choice.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Runtime Choice has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Runtime Choice, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Runtime Choice precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Runtime Choice, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Asteroids team handles case 7269 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Runtime Choice, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Classic pygame and pygame-ce can safely coexist in one environment.

options.text: Declare Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player outcome, revision, runtime, seed, input trace, accessibility profile, and frame, entity, collision, audio, and memory budgets for the workshop Runtime Choice case
Predict Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, failure, pause, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the workshop Runtime Choice case

Change one Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python frame rate, stall, seam, device, collision, cue, save, package, or release condition, verify bounded rejection or repair, and state desktop transfer limits for the workshop Runtime Choice case

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Quit Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-quit-cleanup-8oeguk" that uses case 2252 to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Quit Cleanup.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Quit Cleanup into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Quit Cleanup, keep Quit Cleanup connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-quit-cleanup-8oeguk
# Competency: asteroids-quit-cleanup.
# Case 2252: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_quit_cleanup_8oeguk(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_8oeguk = "8oeguk-kugeo8-2252"
    assert evidence_variant_8oeguk
    evidence_axes_8oeguk = (
        "player8oeguk",
        "seed2252",
        "inputkugeo8",
        "frame8oeguk2252",
        "state22528oeguk",
        "playtestkugeo82252",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_8oeguk)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Quit Cleanup, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Quit Cleanup precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Quit Cleanup, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Asteroids team handles case 6280 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Quit Cleanup, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Closing the terminal is an adequate game shutdown design.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Quit Cleanup
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 7899 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

stimulus.title: Quit Cleanup failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Closing the terminal is an adequate game shutdown design.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Quit Cleanup condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
